profile.name: Gabriel Riven Wahnich
profile.summary: Computer Science graduate with Distinction from RMIT University targeting junior AI Engineer and full-stack software roles. Built and deployed Vestly, a production multi-agent financial analysis platform using OpenAI Agents SDK, MCP/FastMCP, FastAPI, React and PostgreSQL. Shipped REST APIs, authentication systems and tested backend services across internship, freelance and self-directed projects. Bilingual in English and Spanish.

experience.title: Freelance Software Engineer
experience.description: Commissioned pet portrait business platform for browsing artwork, submitting customer enquiries, and managing artwork and testimonial content.
responsibilities: Built a full-stack platform for a commissioned pet portrait business, replacing a static catalogue workflow with customer enquiry flows and admin-managed artwork and testimonial content.

Developed a NestJS backend with 23 REST API endpoints across authentication, image management, testimonials and contact submissions, separating public catalogue routes from JWT-protected ADMIN CRUD operations.
Implemented secure admin workflows using JWT guards, role-based access control, httpOnly access-token cookies and Argon2 password hashing.
Integrated Cloudinary for image storage and delivery, using URL-based transformations and signed-upload endpoints for dynamic resizing, compression and managed media uploads.
Built a responsive frontend using React, Vite, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui and Framer Motion.
Implemented email handling via Resend to manage and route customer enquiries.

experience.title: Software Engineer Intern
experience.description: Early-stage startup building a marketplace platform for musician collaboration.
responsibilities: Owned end-to-end delivery of an order-history feature across the Next.js web application and Expo mobile application, from database schema and backend service logic through to production-ready UI.
Built and maintained the order-history microservice, producing 10 Jest unit tests covering CRUD operations, validation outputs, data flow and web/mobile integration assumptions.
Integrated secure file-download functionality into the order-history interface for cross-artist file sharing.
Collaborated via GitHub using branching, pull requests and code review.

experience.title: Hardware Technician
experience.description: Fast-paced retail and enterprise environment handling custom system builds, hardware diagnostics and customer-facing technical support.
responsibilities: Diagnosed and resolved hardware, firmware, Linux and compatibility faults across consumer and enterprise systems under customer-facing time pressure.
Assembled custom PC builds to specification and performed component-level quality testing.
Applied BIOS and firmware updates to maintain hardware compatibility and system stability.

education.degree: Bachelor of Computer Science with Distinction

projects.name: Vestly
projects.description: A production multi-agent financial analysis platform where Fundamentals, Sentiment, Risk and Competition agents run in parallel and a Judge agent synthesises their findings into an evidence-grounded investment thesis.
details: Built and deployed a FastAPI backend on Fly.io and React/Vite frontend on Vercel.
Architected the agent framework using the OpenAI Agents SDK, Pydantic-validated structured outputs and a shared AnalysisAgent base class.

Developed a dedicated FastMCP server for the Competition agent, exposing peer lookup and research tools that enable autonomous competitor selection and on-demand comparison data retrieval via Finnhub.
Implemented 8 MCP functions/tools, including 5 yFinance tools, 1 Finnhub news tool and 2 competition/research tools.
The yFinance tools fetch company profile data, financials, 1-year price history, analyst recommendations and insider transactions.
The Finnhub tool fetches recent company news from the last 30 days.
The competition and research tools fetch peer companies and generate a full research pack with summary, snapshot, financials, price movement, recent news and confidence scoring.
Produced 27 Pytest unit tests, including 15 tests for confidence-scoring logic and 12 tests for research-pack generation.
Confidence-scoring tests cover missing financials, stale or insufficient news, insufficient price history and missing company metadata.
Research-pack tests cover company summaries, snapshots, financial snapshots, price movement, recent news and full pack creation.

projects.name: AI Portfolio Assistant
projects.description: A deployed AI chat assistant integrated into rivenwahnich.dev that answers questions about Gabriel's professional background using structured tool calls rather than static prompt context.
details: Built with the OpenAI Agents SDK and a custom FastMCP server exposing structured tools for professional experience, projects and technical skills.
Designed the assistant around structured tool calls, allowing the agent to retrieve targeted information about projects, experience and skills.
Packaged the FastAPI application and FastMCP server into a single Fly.io deployment.
Used Pydantic-validated schemas, structured error handling and Dockerised service configuration for consistent production deployment.

interests: Game Development — building small-scale interactive projects with Godot
Football — follower of local A-League and international leagues
